concepts.title: Logarithmic Differentiation
concepts.conceptBody:
Logarithmic Differentiation 

So far, we have discussed derivatives of the functions of the form ${{(f\left( x \right))}^{n}},{{n}^{f\left( x \right)}}$ and ${{n}^{n}}$, where $\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)$ is a function of $\text{x}$ and $\text{n}$ is a constant. In this section, we will be mainly discussing derivatives of the functions of the form ${{(f\left( x \right))}^{g\left( x \right)}}$, where $f\left( x \right)$ and $g\left( x \right)$ are functions of $x$. To find the derivatives of this type of functions we proceed as follows:

Let $y={{(f\left( x \right))}^{g\left( x \right)}}$, taking logarithm of both the sides, we have

$\text{log}y=g\left( x \right)\text{log}\left\{ f\left( x \right) \right\}$

Differentiating w.r.t. $\text{x}$, we get

$\frac{1}{y} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}=g(x) \cdot \frac{1}{f(x)} \cdot \frac{d f(x)}{d x}+\log \{f(x)\} \cdot \frac{d g(x)}{d x}$

$\therefore \quad \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\mathrm{y}\left[\frac{\mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x})}{\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{df}(\mathrm{x})}{\mathrm{dx}}+\log \{\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})\} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{dg}(\mathrm{x})}{\mathrm{dx}}\right]$

or $\quad \frac{d y}{d x}=(f(x))^{g(x)}\left\{\frac{g(x)}{f(x)} \cdot \frac{d f(x)}{d x}+\log \{f(x)\} \cdot \frac{d g(x)}{d x}\right\}$

Logarithmic Differentiating (Objective Approach) 

If $\text{y}={{\{\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)\}}^{\phi \left( \text{x} \right)}}$ then

$\frac{d y}{d x}=$ d.c. of $\{f(x)\}^{\phi(x)} \quad$ w.r.t. $x$ taking $\phi(x)$ as a constant

+ d.c. of $\{\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})\}^{\phi(\mathrm{x})}$ w.r.t. $\mathrm{x}$ taking $\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})$ as a constant.

$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\phi(\mathrm{x}) \cdot\{\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})\}^{\phi(\mathrm{x})-1} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{df}(\mathrm{x})}{\mathrm{dx}}+\{\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})\}^{\phi(\mathrm{x})} \cdot \log \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x}) \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d} \phi(\mathrm{x})}{\mathrm{dx}}$

or when we have to differentiate the function of the form (variable), take log on both sides and differentiate.
